title: Understanding Forces
text: To find the tension in the cable, consider the forces acting on the elevator. The tension in the cable must overcome both the gravitational force and the force due to acceleration.

title: Calculate Gravitational Force
text: The gravitational force acting on the elevator can be calculated using the formula: $$F_{gravity}=m g$$, where $$m$$ is the mass of the elevator and g is the acceleration due to gravity $$(9.8$$ m/s**2).

title: Calculate Gravitational Force
text: What is the gravitational force acting on the elevator? Use the formula $$F_{gravity}=m g$$.

title: Calculate Net Force
text: The net force acting on the elevator is the difference between the tension in the cable and the gravitational force. Use the formula: $$F_{net}=m a$$, where a is the acceleration of the elevator.

title: Calculate Net Force
text: What is the net force acting on the elevator? Use the formula $$F_{net}=m a$$.

title: Calculate Tension in the Cable
text: The tension in the cable can be found by adding the gravitational force to the net force: $$Tension=F_{gravity}+F_{net}$$.

title: Calculate Tension in the Cable
text: What is the tension in the cable during acceleration opposite to the motion? Use the formula $$Tension=F_{gravity}+F_{net}$$.
